title: etcd Key-Value Store
description: Schema for etcd key-value store entities including key-value pairs, leases, cluster members, transactions, watch events, and authentication resources used by the etcd v3 API.

KeyValue.title: KeyValue
KeyValue.description: A key-value pair stored in the etcd cluster. Keys and values are base64-encoded byte strings. The revision fields track the cluster-wide revision at which the key was created and last modified.

create_revision.description: Cluster revision when this key was first created. Used for optimistic concurrency control in transactions.

mod_revision.description: Cluster revision when this key was last modified. Monotonically increasing with each write operation.

version.description: Version of the key, starts at 1 on creation and increments by 1 on each put. Resets to 1 after delete and recreate.

value.description: The stored value in base64-encoded format. Can be any binary data.

lease.description: Lease ID attached to the key. When the lease expires, this key is automatically deleted. Value of 0 means no lease is attached.

ResponseHeader.title: ResponseHeader
ResponseHeader.description: Metadata header included in every etcd API response, containing cluster identification and the store revision at the time of the response.

cluster_id.description: Unique identifier for the etcd cluster

member_id.description: Unique identifier for the cluster member that generated the response

revision.description: Key-value store revision at the time of the response. Monotonically increasing with each write operation.

raft_term.description: Raft term at the time of the response. Increments each time a new leader is elected.

Lease.title: Lease
Lease.description: A time-based lease that can be attached to keys to give them a TTL. When the lease expires, all attached keys are automatically deleted. Clients must periodically send keepalive requests to prevent lease expiration.

ID.description: Unique identifier for the lease assigned by the etcd cluster

TTL.description: Time-to-live in seconds for the lease. After this duration without a keepalive, the lease expires.

grantedTTL.description: Original TTL in seconds that was granted when the lease was created

keys.description: List of keys attached to this lease that will be deleted when the lease expires

Member.title: Member
Member.description: A node participating in the etcd cluster. Members communicate via peer URLs and serve clients via client URLs. Learner members are non-voting participants that receive Raft log replication but do not vote in elections.

ID.description: Unique identifier for the cluster member

name.description: Human-readable name for the cluster member, typically the hostname

peerURLs.description: URLs used by other etcd cluster members to communicate with this member

clientURLs.description: URLs that etcd clients use to connect to this member

isLearner.description: True if this member is a non-voting learner. Learners receive Raft log entries but do not count toward quorum.

WatchEvent.title: WatchEvent
WatchEvent.description: An event describing a change to a key in the etcd cluster. Emitted by watch streams when keys are created, updated, or deleted.

type.description: Type of the event. PUT indicates a key was created or updated; DELETE indicates a key was deleted.

kv.description: The current key-value pair at the time of the event. For DELETE events, only the key and mod_revision are populated.

prev_kv.description: The previous key-value pair before the event occurred. Only present when the watch was created with prev_kv=true.

Permission.title: Permission
Permission.description: An access control permission granting read, write, or read-write access to a specific key or key range in the etcd cluster.

permType.description: Type of access granted. READ allows get/range operations, WRITE allows put/delete operations, READWRITE allows both.

key.description: The key or start of the key range in base64-encoded format

range_end.description: The end of the key range in base64-encoded format (exclusive). If omitted, only the single key is covered.

Role.title: Role
Role.description: An authorization role that defines a set of permissions over key ranges. Roles are assigned to users to grant them access to specific parts of the etcd key space.

name.description: Unique name for the role within the etcd cluster

keyPermission.description: List of key-range permissions associated with this role

User.title: User
User.description: An authenticated user in the etcd authorization system. Users are assigned roles that define their access permissions to the key-value store.

name.description: Unique username for the user within the etcd cluster

roles.description: List of role names assigned to the user

no_password.description: When true, the user has no password and can only authenticate via mTLS client certificates

Alarm.title: Alarm
Alarm.description: A cluster-level alarm indicating an error condition. When a NOSPACE alarm is active, the cluster stops accepting write requests to prevent data loss.

memberID.description: ID of the cluster member that has this alarm

alarm.description: Type of alarm. NOSPACE indicates insufficient storage; CORRUPT indicates data corruption detected.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: An atomic compare-and-swap transaction that executes a set of operations conditionally. All compare conditions must be true for the success branch to execute; otherwise the failure branch runs. The entire transaction is atomic and isolated.

compare.description: Conditions evaluated to determine which branch to execute

success.description: Operations executed when all compare conditions are true

failure.description: Operations executed when any compare condition is false

Compare.title: Compare
Compare.description: A condition in a transaction that compares a key attribute (version, create revision, mod revision, value, or lease) to a target value using a comparison operator.

result.description: The comparison operator to apply when evaluating the condition

target.description: The attribute of the key to compare against the target value

key.description: The key whose attribute is being compared in base64-encoded format

range_end.description: Range end for comparing across a range of keys in base64-encoded format

version.description: Version number to compare against when target is VERSION

create_revision.description: Create revision to compare against when target is CREATE

mod_revision.description: Mod revision to compare against when target is MOD

value.description: Value to compare against when target is VALUE in base64-encoded format

lease.description: Lease ID to compare against when target is LEASE

RequestOp.title: RequestOp
RequestOp.description: A single operation within a transaction branch. Exactly one of the request fields should be set to specify the operation type.

request_range.description: A range query operation to execute within the transaction

request_put.description: A put operation to execute within the transaction

request_delete_range.description: A delete range operation to execute within the transaction

request_txn.description: A nested transaction to execute within the outer transaction
